title: Eidolons Routing Data
description: Machine-readable cortex for `eidolons run` (the mechanical routing kernel). Sibling to roster/index.yaml; mirrors EIDOLONS.md trigger/refuse columns + dispatch thresholds.

default_for_class.description: Capability class this member is the default for when multiple members share it (two-coder routing tiebreak; the named-Eidolon bonus still overrides).

requires_host_tier.description: Optional: the host_tier the project must declare for this member to win the default_for_class tiebreak. When set and the project's host_tier does not match, the routing kernel falls through to the next-ranked candidate. A NAMED member still wins via the name bonus regardless of this gate.

degraded_mode.description: v1.1: mechanical degradation shape on weak/undeclared hosts. 'fanout' = loop-native N-shot; 'sample-select' = N-sample deliberation + rubric-agreement selection. The kernel only surfaces this on the routing artifact; execution belongs to the orchestrator/sandbox.

fallback.description: v1.1: declared conservative peer (an eidolons key) the S1.7 host-tier fallthrough gate routes to instead of the generic next-ranked candidate, when the peer is known and non-refusing.

escalation.description: v1.1: verifier-failed micro-task escalation. The kernel only surfaces this data on the routing artifact; execution belongs to the orchestrator/sandbox.

reroute_to.description: An eidolons key this member escalates to on verifier failure.

trance.description: v1.1: TRANCE model-tier split — computed artifact data (EIDOLONS.md §TRANCE 'model-tier upgrade'). Absent block → the kernel omits model_tier_lead/model_tier_workers (fail-open).
